hero.title: Precision design for kitchens, buildings, and complex spaces.

slides.imageAlt: Commercial kitchen and food service layout
slides.title: Kitchen systems built for real service pressure.
slides.description: Line design, exhaust and make-up air, and equipment schedules aligned so operators open on time—with drawings GCs can build.

slides.title: Model-led coordination that survives the job site.
slides.description: Structured LOD, clash-ready views, and issue logs that keep architects, MEP, and trades on one accountable thread.

slides.title: Architecture tuned to light, flow, and long-term value.
slides.description: From massing studies to interior thresholds, we document intent so builders preserve the quiet details that sell the experience.

slides.title: Facades and cores that read sharp at speed.
slides.description: Test fits, amenity strategy, and envelope logic packaged for permitting and procurement—without losing design discipline.

slides.title: Spaces that balance operation, comfort, and brand.
slides.description: Circulation, adjacencies, and finish intent captured in schedules and details so site teams execute without guesswork.

slides.title: From concept imagery to buildable clarity.
slides.description: Visual narratives for stakeholders, paired with documentation discipline so design intent survives value engineering and construction.

about.title: A studio built for coordination—not surprises.
body: Aim Integrated Design partners with operators, architects, and builders who need clarity from the first sketch to construction documents.
Our process favors tight communication, model-based coordination, and documentation that trades can execute with confidence.

services.title: Services engineered for operators and project teams.
services.subtitle: Hover a card to explore how each discipline supports your timeline and budget.

items.title: Commercial Kitchen Design
items.description: Production-first layouts, equipment matrices, MEP coordination, and health department readiness.

items.title: BIM Modeling
items.description: Parametric models, LOD strategy, clash mitigation, and structured exports for field teams.

items.title: Floor Planning
items.description: Test-fit studies, adjacency diagrams, and capacity planning tuned to real service models.

items.title: Architectural Design
items.description: Concept design, interior architecture, detailing, and documentation with a cohesive visual language.
